title: Peristylus subaphyllus
taxonomy: Peristylus subaphyllus is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Peristylus, which is a group of orchids commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The species is native to Asia, specifically in countries such as China, India, and Southeast Asia. Peristylus subaphyllus is a terrestrial orchid, often growing in damp, shaded areas. Its classification also places it within the subfamily Orchidoideae, one of the larger subfamilies within the Orchidaceae family.

intro: Peristylus subaphyllus, also known as the Asian ground orchid, is a type of terrestrial orchid native to various parts of Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat a range of health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Peristylus subaphyllus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects.
